meaning: n.鞭子的皮条,鞭绳vt.鞭打,反方向猛扭vi.(鞭抽似的)猛动
examples: His wife suffered whiplash and shock.
He was very bruised and suffering from whiplash.
Presently, no objective test for whiplash exists, so it is true that spurious reports of whiplash injuries cannot be readily identified.
You got whiplash if you were expecting the status quo of the long 1970s to continue.
Whiplash claims were a major factor behind the rapid rise in the price of motor cover.
